     The Numbers Business Report

A Million Ways to Die in The West (2014)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $42,720,965Details
International Box Office $44,057,592
Worldwide Box Office $86,778,557
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $9,631,618
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $5,622,528
Total $15,254,146
Further financial details...

Synopsis

After cowardly sheep farmer Albert backs out of a gunfight, his fickle girlfriend leaves him for another man. When a mysterious and beautiful woman rides into town, she helps him find his courage and they begin to fall in love. But when her husband, a notorious outlaw, arrives seeking revenge, the farmer must put his newfound courage to the test.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$16,797,385 (39.3% of total gross)
Legs:2.54 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:49.2% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$40,000,000 (worldwide box office is 2.2 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,158 opening theaters/3,160 max. theaters, 3.5 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $62,538,888

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: May 30th, 2014 (Wide) by Universal
International Releases: May 28th, 2014 (Wide) (Egypt)
May 29th, 2014 (Wide) (Germany)
May 29th, 2014 (Wide) (Netherlands)
May 29th, 2014 (Wide) (New Zealand)
May 29th, 2014 (Wide) (Switzerland)
Video Release: October 7th, 2014 by Universal Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for strong crude and sexual content, language throughout, some violence and drug material.
(Rating bulletin 2318, 4/18/2014)
Running Time: 115 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Digital Cinematography, Directing Yourself, Gratuitous Cameos, Intertitle, Prologue, Scene in End Credits, Screenplay Written By the Star, Training Montage, Voiceover/Narration
Plot point: Dysfunctional Family, Gangs, Hallucinations, Intentionally Anachronistic, Narcotics, Native Americans, Non-Chronological, Prostitution, Relationships Gone Wrong, Romance
Subgenre: Comedy Westerns
Time period setting: 1880s
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Western
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Historical Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Bluegrass Films
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English
